description:
You are a backup-integrity engineer who has just restored a critical
  archive from tape.  
  Your job is to verify the restored data and leave a traceable record
  of the verification in a log file.

  Files/directories already present:
    1. /home/user/backups/daily/important_data.tar.gz  
       (this is the file whose integrity you must check)

  What you have to do:
    1. Compute the SHA-256 checksum of
       /home/user/backups/daily/important_data.tar.gz.
    2. Create the directory /home/user/backup_verification/
       if it does not already exist.
    3. Inside that directory, create the file
       /home/user/backup_verification/2023-07-21_integrity.log
       containing ONE and only one line with the following exact format
       (no extra spaces or tabs):

            important_data.tar.gz:<sha256-hash>

       where <sha256-hash> is the 64-character hexadecimal SHA-256 digest
       you obtained in step 1.
    4. The file must end with a single trailing newline character
       (ASCII 0x0A) and no additional blank lines.
    5. Use only one terminal command to perform the checksum calculation
       and create/write the log file.  (Pipeline operators such as “|”,
       “>”, “>>”, “&&”, or command substitutions are all allowed;
       but it must be entered and executed as a single command line.)

  What the automated test will check:
    • The file /home/user/backup_verification/2023-07-21_integrity.log
      exists.  
    • Its contents consist of exactly one line that matches

          important_data.tar.gz:<64-hex-chars>\n

      where the digest is the correct SHA-256 of
      /home/user/backups/daily/important_data.tar.gz.

  Do NOT hard-code the digest into the log file; the test suite will
  recalculate it independently, so the value in your log must match.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing file and its checksum -->
  <file path="/home/user/backups/daily/important_data.tar.gz"
        sha256="ba7816bf8f01cfea414140de5dae2223b00361a396177a9cb410ff61f20015ad">
    ascii content: "abc"                <!-- 3 bytes, no newline -->
  </file>

  <!-- Expected artefact produced by the agent -->
  <file path="/home/user/backup_verification/2023-07-21_integrity.log">
    expected_content:
      "important_data.tar.gz:ba7816bf8f01cfea414140de5dae2223b00361a396177a9cb410ff61f20015ad\n"
  </file>
name: task_000000_b0dd1716
